decision: RAG is a read-only suggestion and context layer.
rationale: Prevent accidental auto-mutation by ensuring RAG output is always advisory, never executable without human review.
applies_to: All agents, all contours, all RAG usage
forbidden_alternatives: Auto-mutating code based on RAG output
Auto-saving files from RAG suggestions
Auto-applying Product Actions from RAG context

decision: RAG must not auto-mutate any file.
rationale: Facts and BM25 snippets provide context; agents must use their own reasoning to decide whether and how to edit files.
applies_to: All RAG tooling, all agent preflight integrations
forbidden_alternatives: RAG CLI that rewrites source files
RAG server that patches code automatically
Fact-based auto-fix scripts

decision: RAG must not write or mutate BPMN XML.
rationale: BPMN XML is product runtime data. RAG is a context layer and must not touch runtime data formats.
applies_to: All RAG contours, all agent contexts
forbidden_alternatives: RAG-based BPMN XML generation
RAG-based process model auto-modification
Fact-driven BPMN element insertion/deletion

decision: AI drafts are not canonical source truth.
rationale: Drafts, WIP notes, and unreviewed suggestions may contain errors or hypotheses. Only reviewed and verified documents are truth.
applies_to: All agents, all contour reports, all Project Atlas docs
forbidden_alternatives: Indexing draft notes as canonical
Treating AI-generated plans as binding contracts
Using unverified facts as acceptance criteria

decision: Product Actions durable truth source is interview.analysis.product_actions[].
rationale: Product Actions represent user-validated requirements. They must be the authoritative source for feature work, not RAG snippets or agent hypotheses.
forbidden_alternatives: Deriving Product Actions from RAG search results
Inventing product requirements from BM25 snippets
Overriding interview-derived actions with agent guesses

decision: Product Actions must not be written into BPMN XML.
rationale: Product Actions are requirements metadata. BPMN XML is runtime process model data. They belong in separate layers.
applies_to: All backend/API work involving Product Actions and BPMN
forbidden_alternatives: Storing product_actions array inside BPMN XML extensions
Writing action text directly into bpmn:task names

decision: Version marker must not overlay the BPMN canvas.
rationale: Canvas overlay interferes with diagram interaction and degrades user experience.
forbidden_alternatives: Rendering version text inside the bpmn-js canvas container
Placing version badge over the diagram viewport

rationale: Users need clear feedback that a new version has been saved or deployed.
forbidden_alternatives: Silent version increments with no UI feedback
Version changes only visible in backend logs

decision: Large god files require decomposition-first before adding new logic.
rationale: Adding logic to large files increases maintenance burden and risk. Decomposition reduces complexity and enables testing.
applies_to: All backend and frontend code changes
forbidden_alternatives: Adding new features to files >500 lines without decomposition
Bypassing decomposition because of time pressure

decision: For TO-BE format, follow only the user-provided document; no invented terms unless marked hypothesis.
rationale: TO-BE documents represent user intent. Inventing terms introduces misalignment between implementation and requirements.
applies_to: All TO-BE modeling and process design contours
forbidden_alternatives: Adding BPMN elements not present in the user TO-BE document
Renaming process steps without user approval
Presenting agent-invented terms as user requirements
